input.text:
Shift 1 Handoff - RN1

BED 1: Mrs. Patricia Reyes, 84, post-hip surgery (ORIF) day 4. Full code. Allergy: penicillin. Vitals stable (NEWS2 0), alert, no acute changes. Enoxaparin 40 mg SC given. No new issues observed, continue to monitor post-op course.

BED 2 (FOCAL): Mrs. Elena Aquino, 78, post-CAP day 6. DNR, POLST on file, allergy: sulfa. Hx: CHF, T2DM (on furosemide, metformin). Vitals: HR 88, RR 17, SpO2 95%, BP 124, Temp 37.1°C (NEWS2 1). Today, patient is noticeably more pale and withdrawn than previously, picking at breakfast and less engaged with staff. Please observe closely for evolving changes in mental status, worsening intake, or clinical deterioration. All scheduled meds given (azithromycin [last dose], furosemide, metformin). Needs ongoing close monitoring for subtle signs of decline given recent pneumonia and risk factors.

BED 3: Mr. Walter Goldberg, 91, dementia, recurrent UTI. DNR/DNI/DNH, no known allergies. Vitals: HR 92, RR 20, SpO2 93%, BP 110, Temp 37.6°C (NEWS2 3, but at baseline). No acute change from prior days, observed stable. Ceftriaxone 1g IV given.

Summary: All patients medicated and stable. Major concern is Mrs. Aquino’s change in appearance/engagement; please watch mental status and food intake closely this shift.
